A: Define a couple of pointers first and last
to hold the begining and end of the will be
Then do an inorder traversal of the tree, where
each node checks if there is already a first and
last. If not, make first and last equal to the
current node (this will happen when you find the
left-most node in the tree which will become the
first node in the list). Otherwise, do:
Will connect each node to the right of its pre-
decesor. Once the inorder traversal has finished
the first and last pointers must be connected to
A: Declare a MovingAverage class with 3 member
variables: an int maxSize, a window queue and
This way, every MovingAverage instance keeps
at most maxSize elements in its internal window
while keeping windowSum updated to be equivalent
to the sum of all the elements of window at any
To compute the next moving average value, imple-
ment a function next(x) that adds x to the window,
ensures it does not exceed maxSize, updates
windowSum and returns windowSum / len(window) as
the result. This solves the problem.
m.next(1) # Adds 1 to the window and returns 1
m.next(10)# Adds 10 to the window and returns 5.5
m.next(3) # Adds 3 to the window and returns 4.6
m.next(5) # Adds 5 to the window and returns 6
15. 121. Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock (Easy)
A: Traverse the prices array from left to right
keeping track of two values: minPrice and
If at any index i we find a prices[i] < minPrice,
update minPrice. Similarly for maxProfit, if
Return maxProfit at the end of the traversal.
Return 4 as it is was the max profit that could be
achieved from this array of prices.
16. 236. Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Tree
A: This is a variant of an already solved problem
the definition of the Node class included a parent
pointer such that the depth of any given node
could be easily found by counting how many parents
need to be traversed from the node up to the root
of the tree. Since our solution for that problem
relied on knowing the depth of nodes p and q,
However, the definition of Node in this problem
does not include a parent pointer. Thus, we need
to compute the parent for each node. This can be
done by traversing the tree using BFS capturing
the parents in a parents dictionary. From that
moment on, we can proceed as we did with the
previous problem, finding the depths of p and q
and using them to find the lowest common ancestor.
